# Eyes Peeled

> WCAG contrast and accessibility validator. Checks color combinations against WCAG 2.1 standards, generates compliant alternatives while preserving design intent. Uses CIELAB color space for perceptual accuracy.

## What it does

Validates color pairs against WCAG 2.1 (AA/AAA for normal text, large text, and UI components). When combinations fail, generates minimal adjustments using LAB color space to preserve hue and saturation. Audits entire palettes with matrix analysis.

## Core functions

```python
# Check a single pair
result = check_wcag(hex_to_rgb('#2563eb'), hex_to_rgb('#ffffff'))
# Returns: ratio, AA_normal, AA_large, AAA_normal, AAA_large, UI_component

# Generate fixes for failing pairs
fixes = generate_fixes(fg_rgb, bg_rgb, target_ratio=4.5)
# Returns: strategies (darken_foreground, lighten_background, balanced)

# Audit an entire palette
results = audit_palette({'primary': '#3b82f6', 'secondary': '#8b5cf6', ...})
# Returns: all combinations, safe backgrounds/foregrounds, problem colors, stats

# Generate accessible variants
variants = generate_accessible_variants('#60a5fa', '#ffffff', levels=['AA', 'AAA'])
```

## WCAG thresholds

- AA Normal Text: 4.5:1
- AA Large Text: 3:1 (18pt+ or 14pt+ bold)
- AAA Normal Text: 7:1
- AAA Large Text: 4.5:1
- UI Components: 3:1

## Fix strategies

1. Darken foreground only (preserve background)
2. Lighten background only (preserve foreground)
3. Balanced adjustment (both colors move minimally)

All fixes preserve hue and saturation, adjusting only lightness in CIELAB space.

## Part of a suite

Hexed (colors) + Specimen (typography) + Gridlock (layout) + Eyes Peeled (accessibility) + Devourer (components)

Quality gate: run Eyes Peeled after Hexed to validate palette accessibility before generating components with Devourer.
